title: Combat Persistence Decision
summary: Deciding when to continue fighting versus when to disengage.
details: Continue combat when: winning exchange rate, objective requires it, retreat is more dangerous than fighting. Disengage when: losing exchange rate, objective lost, ammunition critical, damage compromising survival. The decision point is often before casualties become irreversible. Better to withdraw with a damaged ship than lose the ship entirely. Consider long-term mission over short-term engagement.
annotations: Pride kills captains who should have withdrawn.
Mission success sometimes requires running away.
